events.title: Photography Walk — MG Road
events.desc: Join fellow photographers for a 2-hour street photography walk along MG Road. All skill levels welcome — bring your camera or phone. We'll review shots together over chai at the end.

recap.summary: Eighteen members met at Trinity Circle for a two-hour street photography walk along MG Road. We split into pairs for candid portraits, practiced framing in harsh midday light, and regrouped at a café for a relaxed critique session over chai. Several members tried long-exposure experiments at the metro crossing for the first time.
highlights: Candid street portraits near Trinity Circle
Mini critique session over chai at the end
Top shots featured in the club gallery this week

events.title: GenAI Design Sprint
events.desc: A fast-paced 90-minute design sprint using generative AI tools. Teams of 3 will tackle a real Adobe brief, present concepts live, and get feedback from senior designers.

recap.summary: Six teams tackled a real Adobe onboarding brief in a fast 90-minute GenAI design sprint. Each group used generative tools for mood boards, micro-copy, and a clickable prototype, then pitched live to senior designers. The winning concept focused on a friendlier first-day checklist for new hires.
highlights: Six teams presented GenAI-assisted concepts live
Winning pitch: onboarding micro-flow for new hires
Slides and prompt templates pinned in the club Slack channel

events.title: Hackathon Kickoff 2025
events.desc: The Dev Guild's annual hackathon kicks off with team formation, problem-statement reveals, and a kick-off talk from a guest engineer. 48 hours of building, mentorship, and snacks ahead.

events.title: Cricket League — Round 2
events.desc: Round 2 of the inter-department cricket league. Come cheer for your team or fill an open spot on the roster. Light refreshments provided after the match.

events.title: Portfolio Showcase Night
events.desc: Designers and illustrators from across the office share their personal projects, side hustles, and experimental work. An open evening of inspiration, critique, and conversation.

events.title: Cuisine of the Month — Kerala Special
events.desc: This month we're exploring Kerala cuisine. Members bring home-cooked dishes, share recipes, and vote on their favourite. A fun, flavourful lunch break that celebrates our diversity.

events.title: June Read — Atomic Habits Discussion
events.desc: Our June pick is Atomic Habits by James Clear. Come ready to discuss your key takeaways, habits you've tried, and which idea challenged you most. No prep required — drop in and listen.

events.title: Strategy Tournament — Settlers of Catan
events.desc: Our most competitive night of the quarter — a Settlers of Catan tournament with a rolling bracket. Beginner tables available. Winner takes the Guild Trophy for July.

events.title: Campus Clean-up Drive
events.desc: A 90-minute campus clean-up to kick off our zero-waste July challenge. Gloves and bags provided. We'll also do a quick audit of single-use plastics in the office and share findings.

events.title: Burnout & Balance — Open Circle
events.desc: A safe, informal conversation circle about work stress and burnout. Facilitated by a trained peer supporter. You can share, listen, or just be present — no pressure either way.

events.title: Wellness Run — Campus Loop
events.desc: A relaxed 5K loop around campus with stretch breaks. All paces welcome — walk, jog, or sprint with colleagues before the day starts.

recap.summary: Forty-two members joined the morning campus loop at three pace groups — walk, jog, and sprint. Hydration stations were set up at the halfway mark, and the wellness team led a short stretch cooldown on the lawn afterward. Average finish time came in under 35 minutes across the faster group.
highlights: Three pace groups from relaxed walk to sprint
Hydration stations at the halfway mark
Post-run stretches led by the wellness team

events.title: Lightning Talks — June Edition
events.desc: Five-minute talks on side projects, tooling tips, and lessons learned. Sign up at the door or just listen in.

events.title: South Indian Breakfast Potluck
events.desc: Members brought idli, dosa, filter coffee, and chutney. Recipe cards swapped and a blind taste test crowned the best sambar.

recap.summary: More than thirty home-cooked dishes filled the cafeteria tables for our South Indian breakfast potluck. Members swapped recipe cards, shared family variations for filter coffee and chutney, and ran a blind taste test to crown the best sambar of the morning.
highlights: Standout entries included neer dosa and Mysore pak
Recipe cards swapped at every table
Blind sambar taste test with a tie-breaker round

SportZone posted the cricket league schedule for June. Sign-ups open!

articles.title: How to start a club at Adobe Bengaluru
articles.excerpt: Everything you need to know about proposing a new club — from finding a sponsor to your first event.
articles.body:
Starting a club at Adobe Bengaluru is easier than you think. Begin by identifying a clear purpose and a group of at least five interested colleagues. Draft a one-page proposal covering the club's mission, planned activities, and meeting cadence.

Next, find an executive sponsor — any director-level leader who can champion your idea. Submit your proposal through the internal Clubs portal; reviews happen every two weeks.

Once approved, you'll receive a Slack channel, a budget line for events, and a spot on the clubs page. Plan your launch event within 30 days to keep momentum high. The Clubs admin team is always available on #adobe-clubs-help for questions.
articles.author: Clubs Admin

articles.title: Running your first club event: a checklist
articles.excerpt: Room bookings, Slack announcements, swag, and follow-ups — the complete pre-event playbook.
articles.body:
A great club event starts two weeks out. Book your room through the internal portal and add it to the shared clubs calendar. Create a Slack post in #adobe-clubs with a clear title, date, time, location, and a one-line hook.

One week before, send a reminder and confirm catering if needed. Prepare a simple sign-in sheet (or use the QR check-in link from the admin team). During the event, take photos — they fuel the gallery page and future recruitment.

Within 48 hours, post a recap with highlights and photos. Tag attendees and thank your volunteers. Log attendance in the clubs dashboard so your club's activity score stays current.
articles.author: Events Team

articles.title: Club budget guidelines for FY25
articles.excerpt: Annual allocations, reimbursement rules, and what expenses are covered for club leads.
articles.body:
Each approved club receives an annual budget based on member count and activity level. Base allocation is ₹15,000 per year; active clubs with 30+ members may qualify for up to ₹35,000.

Eligible expenses include event catering, workshop materials, guest speaker honorariums (up to ₹5,000), and club event supplies. Personal equipment, alcohol, and off-site travel are not reimbursable.

Submit expenses within 30 days with receipts via the internal expense tool, tagged to your club cost centre. Club leads must approve all submissions before finance review. Unused budget does not roll over — plan events across the year.
articles.author: Finance · Clubs

articles.title: Why clubs matter: stories from Bengaluru
articles.excerpt: Members share how Dev Guild, Adobe Lens, and Food & Brew changed their daily work life.
articles.body:
Clubs aren't just extracurricular — they're where cross-team relationships form. Priya from Product Design met her co-founder for a side project at a Dev Guild hackathon. Raj from Engineering discovered portrait photography through Adobe Lens and now shoots every campus event.

Food & Brew's monthly potlucks have become the unofficial onboarding ritual for new hires in Tower B. These connections translate directly into smoother collaboration: people who share hobbies communicate faster on projects.

If you've been on the fence about joining, pick one event this month. Every club welcomes drop-ins — no long-term commitment required.
articles.author: Internal Comms

articles.title: Setting up your club Slack channel the right way
articles.excerpt: Naming conventions, pinned resources, bots, and integrations every club lead should configure.
articles.body:
Your club Slack channel is the hub of community life. Use the naming format #club-[short-name] (e.g. #club-dev-guild). Pin three things on day one: the club charter, the events calendar link, and the code of conduct.

Enable the Google Calendar app so members see upcoming events in-channel. Add the Clubs bot — it posts weekly digests and lets members opt into notifications without spamming the whole office.

Create thread norms: event discussions in threads, announcements as top-level posts. Archive inactive channels after six months of no posts — the admin team can always restore them if the club relaunches.
articles.author: Dev Guild

articles.title: Recruiting members without being annoying
articles.excerpt: Ethical outreach tactics that grow your club sustainably — no spam, no pressure.
articles.body:
The best recruitment is passive visibility. Keep your club page updated with photos from recent events. Ask members to add the club to their internal profile — it shows up in people search.

For active outreach, target new hires in their first 30 days with a personal invite, not a mass CC. Partner with other clubs for joint events — you'll each inherit a slice of the other's audience.

Avoid posting in #general more than once a quarter. Instead, contribute to existing threads where your club's expertise is relevant. Quality over quantity: ten engaged members beat a hundred silent ones.
articles.author: Clubs Admin

articles.title: Photography tips for club event coverage
articles.excerpt: Shot lists, lighting basics, and how to get your photos on the gallery page faster.
articles.body:
Every club event deserves a visual record. Bring a wide-angle lens (or use your phone's 0.5×) for group shots, and get close-ups of hands-on activities — those feel more authentic than posed smiles.

Shoot during golden hour for outdoor events; indoors, stand near windows and avoid overhead fluorescent-only lighting. Capture at least three types: one group, one detail, one candid moment.

Upload to the gallery within a week using the #club-gallery Slack workflow. Add club name, event title, and photographer credit. The comms team features the best shots in the monthly newsletter.
articles.author: Adobe Lens Club

articles.title: Code of conduct for club events
articles.excerpt: Expectations for inclusive, safe gatherings — applies to all clubs and attendees.
articles.body:
All Adobe club events must uphold our workplace standards of respect and inclusion. Harassment, discrimination, and exclusionary behaviour are never tolerated — report incidents to your club lead or HR immediately.

Events with alcohol require prior approval and must include non-alcoholic options. Virtual events should use Adobe-approved platforms only; do not record sessions without explicit consent from all participants.

Club leads are responsible for enforcing these guidelines at every gathering. When in doubt, refer to the full Employee Conduct Policy on the intranet or contact the Clubs admin team.
articles.author: People Team

articles.title: Intern onboarding through clubs
articles.excerpt: How summer interns find community fast — and how club leads can welcome them.
articles.body:
Interns often cite clubs as the fastest way to feel at home in a 3,000-person office. The Volunteering Club and Board Games club see the highest intern turnout because events are low-commitment and social-first.

Club leads: schedule at least one intern-friendly event per quarter — something drop-in, no prep required. Pair interns with a buddy from your club for their first event.

Interns: browse the clubs page in your first week and RSVP to two events outside your team. You'll meet people you'd never encounter in stand-ups — and those connections last beyond the internship.
articles.author: HR · Early Career
